title: NEBOSH IGC
subtitle: International General Certificate
summary: The internationally recognised baseline qualification for HSE practitioners — covers risk, control, ergonomics, fire, and chemical hazards.
outcomes: Apply ILO and ISO 45001 principles in any workplace
Carry out a complete general workplace risk assessment
Investigate accidents and present recommendations to management
Map national / regional regulations onto site practice
modules: Why we should manage workplace health & safety
Managing risk — understanding people and processes
assessment: Two open-book exams (OBE) + a closing interview. Pass mark 45/100.

title: IOSH Managing Safely
subtitle: Supervisor-level benchmark
summary: The benchmark safety qualification for line managers and supervisors — practical, sector-agnostic, finishable in three days.
outcomes: Identify hazards and assess risk to a structured methodology
Apply control-of-work principles to reduce incidents and downtime
Communicate safety responsibilities to a team confidently
Investigate and close out incidents effectively

title: OTHM Level 6 OHS
subtitle: Regulated diploma with MSc progression
summary: Ofqual-regulated Level 6 Diploma in Occupational Health & Safety. Direct entry route to selected MSc top-ups.
outcomes: Lead an organisation-wide OHS management system to ISO 45001
Design and audit safety culture interventions
Articulate the regulatory landscape across multiple jurisdictions
Progress to a UK MSc in Occupational Safety & Health
assessment: Six written assignments + a workplace research project.

title: HABC Level 2 Food Safety
subtitle: Regulated catering qualification
summary: Ofqual-regulated food-safety qualification — mandatory for anyone who handles, prepares or serves food in the UK / GCC.
outcomes: Identify food-safety hazards in a kitchen environment
Apply HACCP principles to a real menu
Maintain personal hygiene and cross-contamination controls
Comply with food labelling and allergen regulations

title: IEMA Foundation
subtitle: Environmental & sustainability
summary: IEMA-approved entry qualification covering ESG fundamentals, carbon literacy and circular-economy thinking.
outcomes: Explain the environmental & sustainability landscape to non-specialists
Read and interpret an organisation's ESG report
Map carbon and resource flows for a typical SME
Identify circular-economy interventions
modules: Climate change and the carbon cycle
Drivers for change — regulation, finance, society

title: ISO 45001 Lead Auditor
subtitle: Management-system audit
summary: Five-day intensive lead-auditor course aligned to ISO 19011 / ISO/IEC 17021 — for HSE consultants and certification-body auditors.
outcomes: Plan and lead an ISO 45001 third-party audit
Apply ISO 19011 audit principles end-to-end
Write audit findings to certification-body standard
Manage non-conformity and corrective-action close-out
